title: The Kingdom and People of Siam, Vol. 1
author: John Bowring
notes: British diplomatic mission account with quantitative descriptions of slavery, war captives, wages, prices, population estimates, and rice production.

title: Report by Her Majesty's Agent and Consul-General in Siam for the Year 1880
author: British Foreign Office / Consul-General in Siam
notes: Trade and political economy report; discusses exports, imports, labor scarcity, public works, monopolies, and slavery.

title: The Kingdom of Siam
author: Siamese Section, Louisiana Purchase Exposition (ed. A. Cecil Carter)
notes: Official Siamese publication with budget, trade, police, regional economic descriptions, and official narrative on slavery.

title: Law for the Abolition of Slavery, Ratanakosindr Sok 124
author: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State (document reproduction)
notes: English rendering of the 1905 abolition law, including date, territorial scope, and four-tical monthly debt credit provision.

title: Siam-French Convention and related diplomatic correspondence
author: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State (document reproduction)
notes: Documents the territorial and jurisdictional settlement with France around 1904-1905.

title: Declaration between the United Kingdom and France concerning Siam, Madagascar, and the New Hebrides
author: British Treaty Series
notes: The April 8, 1904 declaration situating Siam within Anglo-French imperial negotiations.

title: The Thai Census of 1904: Translation and Analysis
author: Volker Grabowsky
notes: Secondary analysis and translation of the 1904 census explanations; crucial for population, ethnicity, and coverage data.

title: Under duress: Lao war captives in Bangkok in the nineteenth century
author: Edward Van Roy
notes: Detailed reconstruction of Lao captive communities, labor specialization, and changing status in Bangkok.

title: Slavery in Nineteenth Century Siam
author: R. B. Cruikshank
notes: Classic interpretive account of slavery, corvée, and abolition chronology.

title: The Coinage of Siam
author: Reginald le May
notes: Used only to document that the tical and baht are equivalent units.
